burial.plot_notes: Cave Hill Cemetery Louisville KY per FA

narrative_blocks.body: Flora Narcissa Eliza Wigginton first married Dr. Albert Hardin of Louisville, who was 'waylaid and murdered fifty years ago on a part of Preston street road, which is now within the city limits' — a violent death that left her a widow; she then married Horace Hooker Jones in Louisville in 1845, her second husband a member of the firm Jones and Halbert. She outlived Horace Jones by thirty-nine years, dying on March 22, 1890 at age eighty-eight or eighty-nine, in perfect health until two weeks before her death, at the home of her grandson Horace J. Hardin at 215 East Broadway, Louisville — her funeral held at Broadway Methodist Church, interment at Cave Hill.

burial.plot_notes: Cave Hill Cemetery Louisville KY per FA; burial delayed to Feb 17, 1852

naming_inspiration.description: Middle name 'Hooker' preserves his mother Hannah Hooker's birth family in the Jones line — she died in 1800 when he was approximately five years old.

narrative_blocks.header: His mother died before he turned six
narrative_blocks.body: Horace Hooker Jones was born January 6, 1795 with 'Hooker' as his middle name, carrying his mother Hannah Hooker's birth family forward into the Jones line. His mother died in May 1800 when he was five; he died in December 1851 at fifty-six in Louisville, Kentucky — a businessman and member of the firm Jones and Halbert — his burial delayed until February 17, 1852 while his widow Flora located a site at Cave Hill Cemetery.

sources.label: Edward Hooker, Descendants of Rev. Thomas Hooker (1909)

burialCemetery.name: Cave Hill Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Major Louisville Kentucky cemetery (founded 1848). Henry Chandler Cowles (HD3787) and his wife Elizabeth Lucretia Waller Cowles (X02786) buried here. Henry Chandler was originally interred at Oak Woods Cemetery, Chicago in September 1939; his remains were transferred to Cave Hill in 1982 to rest beside Elizabeth (per Victor M. Cassidy, 'Henry Chandler Cowles, Pioneer Ecologist,' p. 346). The transfer reunited the family in Elizabeth's hometown.
